papers.name: Paper 2 Problem-solving and Programming
papers.coverage_notes: Problem-solving and programming; pseudocode and one high-level language.

assessment_objectives.name: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding
assessment_objectives.description: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the principles and concepts of computer science.
classification_hints: Items asking for definitions, terminology, recall of principles and concepts.

assessment_objectives.name: Apply knowledge and understanding
assessment_objectives.description: Apply knowledge and understanding of principles and concepts to solve problems.
classification_hints: Items requiring application of principles to given scenarios, calculations, structured questions.

assessment_objectives.name: Analyse, design, code, test and evaluate
assessment_objectives.description: Analyse problems, design solutions, write and test program code, and evaluate outcomes.
classification_hints: Items requiring algorithm design, pseudocode/program writing, testing, evaluation.

question_styles.name: Multiple choice
question_styles.description: Tests breadth of knowledge across topics.

question_styles.name: Programming
question_styles.description: Writing program code in a chosen high-level language.

difficulty_ladder.description: Recall of facts, definitions or single-step application.

lesson_use.notes: Set supported=true only when a teacher/student/parent mode is declared and validated.

marking_guidance.command_words_ref: CAIE IGCSE Computer Science command words (state, describe, explain, write, design) are defined in Section 4 of the syllabus.
marking_guidance.notes: Single-tier qualification (grades A*-G). Pseudocode is the primary programming notation; candidates choose a high-level language for implementation tasks in Paper 2.
